review: Levels are really good but lacks some of the magic of the firsy Opus. Unfortunately the observatory disappeared
10 out of 10, simply perfect and infallible. Nothing else to add, nothing was nmissing, period.
A near perfect platforming game that improves on nearly every aspect of the first game.
Marginally better than the first game, still not particularly interesting. It didn't hold my interest for more than a few hours. All the problems introduced in the first game are still present.
RamRam: I rate it a 10 because it is my favorite Mario game of all time. I like everything from the difficulty, music, levels, and nostalgia I have from the game.
How can video games get more beloved than this? They might as well try, but this will forever be in the hall of fane until abolished.
Masterpiece to this day, even on the switch 2. There is no other game like the mario galaxy games.
In comparison to the first installment, the atmosphere is not that punching, but the gameplay is better and with more interesting ideas. Still a bit linear though.
Expanding on the original while also being great as a stand alone title, totally recommend
The best mario game i play in my life, i start to play this game in the wii when i have 5 years old and its amazing

Incredibly disappointing sequel. I really enjoyed the first game and maybe the first fifth of this game. It quickly rehashes a lot of ideas the first game had and never really feels like a true sequel. It also forces you to do a lot more BS to get to the ending as just finishing all the unique level stars isn't enough, you have to do almost all the prankster stars as well.

Some of the level design is  made for a character that controls much better than Mario. A decent amount of trial and error that is just frustrating. This comes from unclear goals or just poor level design. The game also is a step down in theming. Rosalina's cool space station ship from the first game is gone, and now you have a tiny Mario head that takes about 2 minutes to explore and there isn't much here. Even the level select is lazier as it is just a tradition Mario path through levels instead of the cool galaxy view they had in the first game.

For the boss fights, like the first game, a lot of repeats. Some stage fights are decent and unique, but the big Bowser fights all repeat. Even the last fight is a repeat fight, just with one extra bonus easy phase to it. But thinking back, Bowser fights have always been repeated in these 3D Mario games and never that good really.

Overall, while there are some decent levels and the game looks great for a Wii game, there is just an abundance of issues here I cannot overlook. Even the decent levels are like B, B-'s. I was rating levels in my head and I think maybe 1 level out of the 71 stars I got was an A tier level. Lots of D's in there and that doesn't make a great game. 6.3/10

A fantastic follow-up to one of my favorite games of all time! I slightly prefer the first one, probably because it was so revolutionary, and felt so new.
But the inclusion of Yoshi as well as many new innovative stages, makes this game as much of a must play as the first one!

It's an improvement on its predecessor in every way, and I appreciate the ideas behind it, it's clearly one of the best platformers ever made. However, being forced to use the Wii's motion controls didn't convince me. Sometimes it felt imprecise and got tiring, plus I've noticed I'm less and less inclined to play platformers, I don't enjoy them as much as I used to.

Super Mario Galaxy 2 is one of the finest 3D platformers ever made—packed with unforgettable moments, clever design, and that unmistakable Mario magic. It's a must-play for fans of the genre and one of the best Mario games of all time.

A really good game I love it as well been just I'd still prefer the first one more though but I still think this game is still a must play the first one is like Mankind's greatest creation and the two is like the sequel to Mankind's Greatest creation
Platform masterpiece. It improves on its predecessor in every way, with sublime, versatile, and more challenging gameplay, a mesmerizing soundtrack, and even greater world variety.
I had the biggestblast playing this i will adore this game so much i love this with all my heart

Super mario galaxy is the 2010 game of the year here at the golden gamepad. While not as innovative as the first game. The level design, length and difficulty has greatly improved. Even if this game is really good i do wish nintendo would have done more to make it diffrent from the first game but its still a solid platformer.

Love this game so much it's my childhood and just a great game it has brutal graphics for the year that game came on the Marketplace. And just really love the items the galaxys just everything so it's a 10 out of 10.

I still can’t understand how this game gets such high reviews. It’s by far the weakest 3D Super Mario ever made. Boring, repetitive, and soulless. Having to go through the same levels more than once is just painful. There’s no story, and it feels like a messy collection of random stages with no direction at all. Such a shame, because the first one was absolutely wonderful.
Masterpiece **** and soundtrack, I created an account just to leave the review. This game and the first one defined my childhood, it is truly something very special.
Did amazing job as a sequel of an already wonderful title. We need Super Mario Galaxy 3 and soon! Like please Nintendo

An incredible sequel to an already incredible game. Still has amazing gameplay, controls, galaxies, and I’ll admit a better post game with the green stars. I do prefer the first game because of the story since those cinematic cutscenes are just incredible to watch. I do understand why someone would prefer this one though and I would recommend playing this.

This game is one of the few sequels that surpasses its predecessor. Such an excellent game. Level desiign at its peak, the gameplay is better, the graphics are pretty darn good, the atmosphere had more color variety, the bosses are spectacular and much better, the pacing was faster... Most of the game is better. The best Nintendo game of the 2010 decade after Breath of the Wild.
This is one of the greatest games I have ever played in my life. It is unbelievably fun, the levels are creative, it is just overall such a great and memorable experience.
Obviously the Best game From the History, it had to have a sequel, and of course, Is This is getting better The Gameplay
It really feels like I’m dreaming. I’m fascinated. It feels like the best game that could be made using the Wii Remote’s shaking motion.
I always wanted this and when I first got it, I fell in love with it. It is an upgraded version of Super Mario Galaxy 1, and it is perfect for me.
Every level is oozing with originality and fun it's gems like this that proves why Mario is gaming number 1 now enough of this review i need to charge my WII

This game is undoubtably one of the best games to ever exist, the story is very ... Mario. But the world design is beautiful and fun. In my Opinion, Super Mario Galaxy 2 is a Playworthy game!

Buying is definitely recommended!
Definitely a huge improvement over the lackluster title thats mario galaxy 1 i like the new yoshi gimmick and the level design is definitely improved but this game gets boring after a few minutes
galaxy but they turned into new mario bros and removed all the charm, a painfully underwhelming game and yes even tho his on the cover art, yoshi is barely playable in most levels.

What do you get when the sequel to an already masterfully crafted game comes to life, you get something even better. Mario is back and this time there is double the stars (242), a new slew over power-ups, and Yoshi is here too.

This game is just as amazing as the first game and offers even more galaxies and a better post game I will admit with the green stars. However, I’ll always prefer the first game just because the story is just so good in that game and I think it has better galaxies.
A sequel that is just as good as its predecessor. It offers more of the Mario Galaxy we love, with equally fun levels, great music, and graphics that still hold up to this day.
I found this better than the first game. The gameplay was extremely well done, and it's definitely a fun game to play with family members!

Incredibly disappointing sequel. I really enjoyed the first game and maybe the first fifth of this game. It quickly rehashes a lot of ideas the first game had and never really feels like a true sequel. It also forces you to do a lot more BS to get to the ending as just finishing all the unique level stars isn't enough, you have to do almost all the prankster stars as well.

Some of the level design is  made for a character that controls much better than Mario. A decent amount of trial and error that is just frustrating. This comes from unclear goals or just poor level design. The game also is a step down in theming. Rosalina's cool space station ship from the first game is gone, and now you have a tiny Mario head that takes about 2 minutes to explore and there isn't much here. Even the level select is lazier as it is just a tradition Mario path through levels instead of the cool galaxy view they had in the first game.

For the boss fights, like the first game, a lot of repeats. Some stage fights are decent and unique, but the big Bowser fights all repeat. Even the last fight is a repeat fight, just with one extra bonus easy phase to it. But thinking back, Bowser fights have always been repeated in these 3D Mario games and never that good really.

Overall, while there are some decent levels and the game looks great for a Wii game, there is just an abundance of issues here I cannot overlook. Even the decent levels are like B, B-'s. I was rating levels in my head and I think maybe 1 level out of the 71 stars I got was an A tier level. Lots of D's in there and that doesn't make a great game. 6.3/10

But the inclusion of Yoshi as well as many new innovative stages, makes this game as much of a must play as the first one!

It's an improvement on its predecessor in every way, and I appreciate the ideas behind it, it's clearly one of the best platformers ever made. However, being forced to use the Wii's motion controls didn't convince me. Sometimes it felt imprecise and got tiring, plus I've noticed I'm less and less inclined to play platformers, I don't enjoy them as much as I used to.

Super Mario Galaxy 2 is one of the finest 3D platformers ever made—packed with unforgettable moments, clever design, and that unmistakable Mario magic. It's a must-play for fans of the genre and one of the best Mario games of all time.

I still can’t understand how this game gets such high reviews. It’s by far the weakest 3D Super Mario ever made. Boring, repetitive, and soulless. galaxy but they turned into new mario bros and removed all the charm, a painfully underwhelming game and yes even tho his on the cover art, yoshi is barely playable in most levels.
Marginally better than the first game, still not particularly interesting. It didn't hold my interest for more than a few hours. All the problems introduced in the first game are still present.

Incredibly disappointing sequel. I really enjoyed the first game and maybe the first fifth of this game. It quickly rehashes a lot of ideas the first game had and never really feels like a true sequel. It also forces you to do a lot more BS to get to the ending as just finishing all the unique level stars isn't enough, you have to do almost all the prankster stars as well.

Some of the level design is  made for a character that controls much better than Mario. A decent amount of trial and error that is just frustrating. This comes from unclear goals or just poor level design. The game also is a step down in theming. Rosalina's cool space station ship from the first game is gone, and now you have a tiny Mario head that takes about 2 minutes to explore and there isn't much here. Even the level select is lazier as it is just a tradition Mario path through levels instead of the cool galaxy view they had in the first game.

For the boss fights, like the first game, a lot of repeats. Some stage fights are decent and unique, but the big Bowser fights all repeat. Overall, while there are some decent levels and the game looks great for a Wii game, there is just an abundance of issues here I cannot overlook. Even the decent levels are like B, B-'s. I was rating levels in my head and I think maybe 1 level out of the 71 stars I got was an A tier level. Lots of D's in there and that doesn't make a great game. 6.3/10

galaxy but they turned into new mario bros and removed all the charm, a painfully underwhelming game and yes even tho his on the cover art, yoshi is barely playable in most levels.
Marginally better than the first game, still not particularly interesting. It didn't hold my interest for more than a few hours. All the problems introduced in the first game are still present.

Incredibly disappointing sequel. I really enjoyed the first game and maybe the first fifth of this game. It quickly rehashes a lot of ideas the first game had and never really feels like a true sequel. It also forces you to do a lot more BS to get to the ending as just finishing all the unique level stars isn't enough, you have to do almost all the prankster stars as well.

Some of the level design is  made for a character that controls much better than Mario. A decent amount of trial and error that is just frustrating. This comes from unclear goals or just poor level design. The game also is a step down in theming. Rosalina's cool space station ship from the first game is gone, and now you have a tiny Mario head that takes about 2 minutes to explore and there isn't much here. Even the level select is lazier as it is just a tradition Mario path through levels instead of the cool galaxy view they had in the first game.

For the boss fights, like the first game, a lot of repeats. Some stage fights are decent and unique, but the big Bowser fights all repeat. Even the last fight is a repeat fight, just with one extra bonus easy phase to it. But thinking back, Bowser fights have always been repeated in these 3D Mario games and never that good really.

Overall, while there are some decent levels and the game looks great for a Wii game, there is just an abundance of issues here I cannot overlook. Even the decent levels are like B, B-'s. I was rating levels in my head and I think maybe 1 level out of the 71 stars I got was an A tier level. Lots of D's in there and that doesn't make a great game. 6.3/10

I still can’t understand how this game gets such high reviews. It’s by far the weakest 3D Super Mario ever made. Boring, repetitive, and soulless. Having to go through the same levels more than once is just painful. There’s no story, and it feels like a messy collection of random stages with no direction at all. Such a shame, because the first one was absolutely wonderful.
